title: Flavorful Chinese Wood Ear Mushrooms Noodles
description: A salty, crumbly, and cozy Chinese dish featuring wood ear mushrooms and a pasta water emulsion, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Soak the dried wood ear mushrooms in hot water for 20 minutes, then drain and slice them into thin strips.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions, then set them aside.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced garlic and ginger to the skillet and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the sliced wood ear mushrooms to the skillet and stir-fry until they are tender and lightly browned.
Add the soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil to the skillet and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add the cooked noodles to the skillet and stir-fry until they are well coated with the sauce.
Season the noodles with salt and pepper to taste, then garnish with scallions.
Serve the noodles hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
